original: NASA's 1st Mars rover launched from Cape Canaveral
The Unity Module, the second module of the International Space Station, is launched
An adult giant squid is caught on video by Kubodera near the Ogasawara Islands, 1,000 km (620 miles) south of Tokyo
NASA launches Gemini 7 withFrank BormanandJim Lovell- later the focus of the first crewed space rendezvous
Bob Gage ties NFL record of a 97 yard touchdown run
Military Working Dog "Nemo" saves the life of his handler Airman Robert A. Throneburg during the Vietnam War, surviving a gunshot wound to the nose
An impromptu recording session by the "Million-Dollar Quartet" -Elvis Presley,Carl Perkins,Jerry Lee Lewis, andJohnny Cash- takes place at Sun Studios in Memphis, Tennessee

Montreux Casino, in Montreux, Switzerland, burns down to the ground after a fan fires a flare gun during concert byFrank Zappaand The Mothers of Invention; incident inspires rock band Deep Purple's song "Smoke on the Water"
Caitlin Clarkposts 22 points, 10 rebounds, and 10 assists for Iowa against Wisconsin, surpassing the Big Ten career triple-double record
Garfield Wood, American inventor, motorboat builder and racer (world water speed record x 5; first to travel over 100 mph on water), born in Mapleton, Iowa (d. 1971)
Australian swimmer Murray Rose sets world record 4:27.3 to claim the 400m gold medal at the Melbourne Olympics; Rose later also wins 1,500m gold
New Zealand cricket spin bowler Ajaz Patel (10-119) joins Jim Laker and Anil Kumble as the only three men to take all ten wickets in a Test innings, on Day 2 of the 2nd Test against India in Mumbai
Mt Semeru, Indonesia’s highest volcano, erupts releasing gas clouds and rivers of lava on the island of Java
Hen in Rome lays a uniquely patterned egg, later believed to have predicted the arrival of the Kirch/Newton "Great Comet of 1680"
First successful birth resulting from uterus transplant from a deceased donor in São Paulo, Brazil
North American migratory birds getting smaller, their wings wider due to climate change according to study by University of Michigan published in journal "Ecology Letters"
Thirty-eight colonists from Berkeley Parish, England, disembark in Virginia and give thanks to God, considered by many the first Thanksgiving in the Americas
Wassily Kandinsky, Russian-French abstract painter (Dreamy Inspiration), born in Moscow (d. 1944)
Roberta Lynn Bondar, Canadian neurologist and astronaut (STS 42), born in Sault Sainte Marie, Ontario
The Beatles release "Beatles For Sale", their fourth studio album in the UK
Robert Adler, Austrian-American inventor (remote control for television), born in Vienna, Austria (d. 2007)
PopePaul VIcloses second session of 2nd Vatican Council
Pioneer Venus 1 goes into orbit around Venus
The United Nations warns that the world is on course for the warmest year since records began
New Zealand cricket captain Kane Williamson smashes 251 to set up the home side's innings & 134 run win over the West Indies in the 1st Test in Hamilton
Veronica Taylor [McInerney], American voice actress (Pokemon - Ash Ketchum), born in New York City
Mike Barrowman, American swimmer (Olympic gold 200m breaststroke 1992, World C'ship gold 1991), born in Costa Mesa, California
Boston Bruins' right wing Andy Hebenton sets a new NHL record by playing in his 581st consecutive game in 2-2 tie with Chicago Black Hawks; reaches 630 straight games

NY's Museum of Modern Art hangsHenri Matisse's picture "Le Bateau" the right side up, after stockbroker Genevieve Habert notices it is displayed upside down, the first of 116,000 people in 47 days to notice
Oldest still-operating NHL franchise is officially established as J. Ambrose O’Brien and Jack Laviolette create the “Club de Hockey Canadien,” known today as the Montreal Canadiens
Edmonton center Jimmy Carson becomes just the third player to score 6 NHL hat tricks before the age of 21 in the Oilers 10-6 win over the New York Rangers
Research published in "Nature" and "Science" claims Native Americans had just one migration from Siberia to the New World, at most 23,000 years ago
Ronald M. Sega, American professor of systems engineering and astronaut (STS 60, 76), born in Cleveland, Ohio
Jonathan Goldstein, American actor (Drake & Josh), born in Manhattan, New York
General Washington bids officers farewell at Fraunce's Tavern, NYC
Alpha Phi Alpha, first Black Greek Letter Fraternity, forms
Ownership group Seattle Hockey Partners led by billionaire businessman Davis Bonderman is unanimously granted an NHL license by the Board of Governors; to start play 2020-21 season at KeyArena
Mt Semeru erupts on Java island, Indonesia, killing at least 14 and injuring 56
Duke Ellingtonopens at the Cotton Club in Harlem, NYC
Jeanne Manford, American gay rights activist, born in Flushing, New York (d. 2013)
Albert Bandura, Canadian-American psychologist (social learning theory, Bobo doll experiment), born in Alberta, Canada (d. 2021)
Bernard King, American Basketball Hall of Fame small forward (NBA All-Star 1982, 84-85, 91; NY Knicks, Washington Bullets), born in Brooklyn, New York
Sergei Starikov, Russian ice hockey defenseman and coach (Olympics gold 1984, 88; silver 1980; New Jersey Devils), born in Chelyabinsk, Russia
(Fereydun) "Fred" Armisen, American comedian (Saturday Night Live, 2002-13), actor, writer (Portlandia), and musician (8G Band), born in Hattiesburg, Mississippi
Corliss Williamson, American basketball forward (NBA C'ship 2004, Detroit Pistons; NBA 6th Man of the Year 2002; NCAA Final 4 MOP 1994), born in Russellville, Arkansas
Frank Friday Fletcher is first US admiral to receive Congressional Medal of Honor
Father Marquette builds first dwelling in what is now Chicago
Peter Gaillard of Lancaster, Pennsylvania, patents a horse-drawn mower
Britain outlaws "suttee" in India, a Hindu practice where a widow burns herself to death on her husband's funeral pyre
American Anti-Slavery Society formed by Arthur Tappan in Philadelphia
Manila paper (made from sails, canvas and rope) patented in Massachusetts
Explorer Henery Morton Stanley's expedition reaches Bagamoyo in Indian Ocean
Webb Hayes, son of US President Rutherford Hayes, receives medal of honor
1st Pro football playoff game Buffalo-7, Canton-3 at Polo Grounds, NYC
Baseball approves a free-agent draft
Typhoon Bopha makes landfall in the Philippines, killing at least 81 people
Treaty of Paris: English KingHenry IIIand French KingLouis IXend 100 years of conflict between the Capetian and Plantagenet dynasties
Ottoman SultanSuleiman the Magnificentoccupies Baghdad
Dmitri Shostakovich's 2nd Symphony premieres in Moscow
Aaron Copland's andJerome Robbins' ballet "The Pied Piper" premieres in New York City
